summary.key_finding: An almost-identical course detail page already exists at apps/web/src/app/yoga-courses/online-300-hour-ytt/page.tsx using nearly every required section component. This build is overwhelmingly a re-composition + content-prop swap, not a from-scratch implementation.

components.rationale: Shared component per project memory. Already imported by every page. Do not redesign — render <SiteHeader /> with no props.
SimpleBreadcrumb already supports BreadcrumbItemData[] with slash separator + light tone; CourseHeroSection accepts the BreadcrumbItem[] shape directly via its breadcrumb prop.

CourseHeroSection already implements the exact pattern: left text column (breadcrumb + split lead/accent title + subtitle + CTA + meta-chip grid) and right hero image. Props (breadcrumb, titleLead, titleAccent, subtitle, meta, ctaLabel, ctaHref, heroImage) map 1:1 to the decomposition spec. Title may be split into lead/accent at the build-planner step.

Meta-chip tiles are rendered inline inside CourseHeroSection (icon + label, h-[104px] tile). The standalone ui/course-meta-chip.tsx pill exists for inline pill use cases (e.g. CourseCard) — but the hero uses the inline tile variant, so no separate primitive is required.

Themed CVA button with mint/default/pill variants. CourseHeroSection already renders an inline styled Link CTA — no separate Button instance needed for the hero. Available for any other CTA that's not part of an existing section.

CourseSectionNav is a sticky in-page tab bar with IntersectionObserver-driven active state and pill highlight on active item — exactly the spec. Pass items=[{label, href}]. Sticky top offset is built-in (top-[88px]).

Eyebrow + heading is built into every existing course section (CourseOverviewSection, HighlightsSection, CurriculumSection, etc.) — no standalone SectionHeading primitive needed. If a pure-primitive is desired later, components/ui/section-header.tsx already exists (eyebrow + title + subtitle + align center/left).
CourseOverviewSection.paragraphs[] prop renders the multi-paragraph body. No additional ProseBlock primitive needed.
HighlightsSection already renders eyebrow + heading.

HighlightCard = 52x52 icon tile (bg-brand-lite) + title + body. Identical to the Figma feature card. Rendered via HighlightsSection's items[] (icon string key + title + body). New icon keys (yoga-meets-flight, leaf-filled, build-strength) need to be added to the ICON_RESOLVER map in highlights-section.tsx.
Built into CurriculumSection.
SyllabusCard matches the ~294x148 small card spec (rounded-lg border bg-surface-1, title + body, min-h 148px). Rendered via CurriculumSection.items[].
ScrollNextButton = 46x46 circular brand-primary button with ArrowUpRight icon — matches Figma spec exactly. Already wired into CurriculumSection via the nextHref prop.
Built into CourseEligibilitySection.
ChecklistItem (pill tone, default) = bordered card row with 22x22 check badge + label — pixel-matches Figma. Rendered via CourseEligibilitySection.items[].
Next.js Image is the existing image primitive. CourseEligibilitySection renders the right-column image internally via its rightImage prop.

CourseEligibilitySection is the exact two-column (left checklist + right image) section already used on the existing 300-hour course page. Pass eyebrow='Who Is This For', heading='Is This Course Right for You?', items=[...8 bullets], rightImage='...'.
Built into CertificationBannerSection.
CertificationBannerSection = eyebrow + h2 + dark green band (bg-brand-green-darkest) containing panelHeading + panelParagraphs[]. Exact match for the Figma 'Globally Recognised' dark band.
Built into TestimonialsSection (eyebrow + heading + optional description, centered).

TestimonialCard renders quote + avatar + authorName + authorMeta — the data shape matches. However the current TestimonialsSection lays cards in a uniform 3-col grid (md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3); Figma shows a mosaic layout (1 lg hero card 440x318 + 4 sm cards 440x158) with a `size` prop. Two options: (a) add an opt-in `layout='mosaic'` prop to TestimonialsSection that recognizes a per-item `size: 'lg'|'sm'` field and renders a non-uniform grid (recommended — additive opt-in, default unchanged); (b) render the mosaic at the page level using TestimonialCard directly. The TestimonialCard itself can stay as-is; the section is what needs the additive size-aware layout.

extension_proposal.additive_per_item_prop: size?: 'lg' | 'sm' on TestimonialItem (only consumed when layout='mosaic')
extension_proposal.notes: Do not change any existing default. The current TestimonialsSection is shared; the mosaic must be a strictly additive opt-in.

components.rationale: Built into PreRequisitesSection (centered eyebrow + h2).

PreRequisitesSection already centers the eyebrow + h2 + checklist column (max-w-640 mx-auto, text-center). Uses ChecklistItem rows. Pass items=[...3 unique items]; do not pass the duplicates from Figma.
Built into InstructorsSection.

InstructorCard = 98x98 avatar + name + role/affiliation — exact match for the Figma 4-up instructor row. Rendered via InstructorsSection.instructors[]. Use layout='scroller' (default) or 'grid' depending on responsive target.
Built into FaqSection.

FaqItem is the existing accordion row with +/− indicator, defaultOpen support, framer-motion expand/collapse. FaqSection renders the list of items. Pass items=[{question, answer, defaultOpen: true for first}].
Built into PopularCoursesSection (centered eyebrow + heading + subhead).

ProgramCard (rendered via PopularCoursesSection) = image, title, meta row (icon + label, dot separators), instructor row (avatar OR initials + name), CTA link with arrow. Maps 1:1 to the Figma 3-up popular-course grid. Note: there's also a separate components/ui/course-card.tsx — that one is a horizontal price/CTA card used elsewhere; the right primitive for this section is ProgramCard via PopularCoursesSection.

Shared component per project memory. Used on 5+ pages. Pass eyebrow='Bodhi', headingLead='Begin where', headingAccent='you are.', subhead='...', primaryCta={label, href}. Default theme='dark' matches the Figma dark band. Do not redesign.

Shared component per project memory. Use <SiteFooterBlock showCta={false} /> (matches the pattern in the existing 300-hour page). The HOME_FOOTER_BRAND / HOME_FOOTER_COLUMNS / HOME_FOOTER_LEGAL constants are exported and provide default content. Do not redesign.

shared_component_guardrails: site-header.tsx — render as <SiteHeader />, no prop mutations, no style overrides.
site-footer-block.tsx — render as <SiteFooterBlock showCta={false} />, no default mutations.
closing-cta-section.tsx — pass content props only; do not introduce new variants here.
faq-item.tsx — accordion already supports defaultOpen and +/− indicator; do not redesign.
button.tsx — use existing variants only (default, mint, outline, etc.).

TestimonialsSection — the mosaic layout proposed for testimonials must be an ADDITIVE opt-in prop (layout='mosaic'); default 'grid' behaviour must remain unchanged for the 5+ pages currently consuming it.
warnings: An almost-complete reference implementation already exists at apps/web/src/app/yoga-courses/online-300-hour-ytt/page.tsx. The build-planner should diff the Figma decomposition against that page to decide whether this task is (a) a new course page using the same section stack with new content, or (b) iteration on the existing page.

Figma testimonials section uses a 2x3 mosaic (1 lg + 4 sm) with per-item size. Current TestimonialsSection is a uniform 3-col grid used on multiple pages — extending it requires an additive opt-in prop per project scoping rules.

Two components/ui/*-card components share similar names: course-card.tsx (horizontal price/CTA card) vs program-card.tsx (the 3-up grid card). For the 'more-courses' Figma section the right primitive is ProgramCard (via PopularCoursesSection).

Highlights section icon keys in Figma (yoga-meets-flight, leaf-filled, build-strength) are not in the existing ICON_RESOLVER map in highlights-section.tsx — build-planner needs to either add these to the resolver or remap to existing keys (yoga, leaf, strength).
Figma 'Circulum' label is a typo — keep as Figma copy if literal-match is required, but the prop/href key should be 'curriculum'.
